title: Product Decomposition: ZOLLERN Steel Profiles Capability

sections.title: Christensen Abstraction Framework
sections.content:
ZOLLERN's Steel Profiles division is a **capability platform** — not a fixed product. The Christensen decomposition therefore applies at the capability level: what fundamental mechanism enables value creation, and how does that mechanism map to configurable features?

| Layer | Christensen Term | ZOLLERN Equivalent | Description |
|-------|-----------------|-------------------|-------------|
| L0 | Core mechanism | Plastic deformation of steel | Forcing heated or cold steel through shaped dies/rolls to permanently reshape cross-section |
| L1 | Functional promise | Deliver precision steel cross-sections with controlled geometry | The universal value proposition across all markets |
| L2 | Feature envelope | Configurable geometry + material + surface + hardness | The set of adjustable parameters a customer can specify |
| L3 | Product instance | e.g., T89/B elevator guide rail in C45 | A specific configuration sold to a specific market |
| L4 | Application context | Elevator cabin guidance system | The end-use system where the profile operates |

sections.title: Core Mechanism: Plastic Deformation
sections.content:
The fundamental physics mechanism is **plastic deformation** — steel is forced through a shaped aperture (die or roll gap) at temperatures above or below its recrystallization temperature, permanently reshaping its cross-section while maintaining a constant profile along the bar length. [SRC: web_search]

| Process | Temperature | Deformation Type | Grain Structure Effect | Resulting Properties |
|---------|------------|------------------|----------------------|---------------------|
| Hot rolling | 1,050-1,250 C | Above recrystallization | Refined, equiaxed grains | Good ductility, moderate tolerance |
| Cold drawing | Ambient | Below recrystallization | Elongated, work-hardened | High strength, tight tolerance, smooth surface |
| Cold rolling | Ambient | Below recrystallization | Directional grain flow | Precision flat geometry, good surface |

### Why This Mechanism Creates Value

1. **Near-net-shape**: The profile exits the die/roll with geometry close to final dimensions, eliminating 40-80% of the machining waste that would occur if starting from rectangular bar stock [SRC: web_search]
2. **Grain flow alignment**: Material grain follows the profile contour, improving fatigue life vs. machined-from-block parts by 15-30% [SRC: web_search]
3. **Continuous production**: Bar lengths of 6-12m produced continuously, enabling high material yield per setup
4. **Repeatability**: Once tooling is qualified, every meter of profile is dimensionally identical

sections.title: Feature Envelope (L2 Decomposition)
sections.content:
The capability platform exposes the following configurable features to any market:

| Feature | Parameter | Range | Unit | Constraint |
|---------|-----------|-------|------|-----------|
| Cross-section geometry | Shape | Circular, polygon, T, L, Z, custom | - | 2D only, no 3D variation along length |
| Cross-section area | Area | 5 - 7,650 | mm2 | Cold drawing max 2,500 mm2 |
| Dimensional precision | Tolerance | 0.02 - 0.5 | mm | Tighter = cold process only |
| Surface finish | Roughness Ra | 0.4 - 6.3 | um | Below 0.8 requires grinding |
| Mechanical strength | Tensile strength | 350 - 2,100 | MPa | Grade + process dependent |
| Surface hardness | Hardness HRC | N/A - 64 | HRC | Requires induction hardening, C >= 0.35% |
| Bar length | Length | 0.1 - 12 | m | Cold drawn max 6m |
| Material grade | Steel type | 40+ grades | - | Steel/stainless only, no non-ferrous |

sections.title: Functional Promise Derivation
sections.content:
From the mechanism and feature envelope, the functional promise emerges:

> **"Deliver precision steel cross-sections with controlled geometry, dimensional accuracy, surface quality, and mechanical properties — configured to application requirements."**

This promise is universal across all markets. What varies per market is which subset of the feature envelope matters most.

sections.title: Market-Specificity Matrix
sections.content:
| Feature | Elevator Rails | Hydraulic Cylinders | Linear Guides | Firearms | Automotive |
|---------|---------------|--------------------|--------------|---------|-----------| 
| Geometry complexity | HIGH (T-profile) | LOW (round) | MEDIUM (flat) | HIGH (custom) | MEDIUM (custom) |
| Tolerance criticality | HIGH (guidance) | HIGH (sealing) | VERY HIGH | HIGH (bore) | MEDIUM |
| Surface finish need | MEDIUM | HIGH | VERY HIGH | HIGH | MEDIUM |
| Hardness need | HIGH (rail head) | LOW | HIGH | MEDIUM | MEDIUM |
| Volume per order | HIGH (km of rail) | MEDIUM | LOW | LOW | HIGH |
